businessModel.text: Revenue from three segments: Infrastructure (hybrid cloud, mainframes, 35%), Consulting (Red Hat, HashiCorp automation, 30%), and Software (watsonx AI, enterprise databases, 35%). annuity maintenance and professional services.

recentCatalysts.text: Granular AI and data platform growing double-digit; IBM's watsonx portfolio gaining enterprise traction Mainframe renewal cycle remains active — Z-system software revenue provides visible annuity base HashiCorp acquisition (mid-2024) deepens infrastructure-automation moat Spin-off of Kyndyl completed Q3 2025, sharpening business profile
items: Granular AI and data platform growing double-digit; IBM's watsonx portfolio gaining enterprise traction
Mainframe renewal cycle remains active — Z-system software revenue provides visible annuity base
Spin-off of Kyndyl completed Q3 2025, sharpening business profile

keyRisks.text: 1. Hybrid cloud growth has slowed from peak; watsonx AI services still 2. Revenue has been flat-to-declining for six consecutive quarters (FY24 total $62.8bn) 3. High-margin mainframe software growth is dependent on legacy upgrade cycle continuation 4. Acquisition integration risk: HashiCorp and Kyndyl drag on operating margins 5. Skilled-labour intensity in professional services constrains margin expansion

risks.risk: 1. Hybrid cloud growth has slowed from peak; watsonx AI services still <5% of revenue
2. Revenue has been flat-to-declining for six consecutive quarters (FY24 total $62.8bn)
3. High-margin mainframe software growth is dependent on legacy upgrade cycle continuation
4. Acquisition integration risk: HashiCorp and Kyndyl drag on operating margins
5. Skilled-labour intensity in professional services constrains margin expansion

whoShouldOwn.text: Long-term infrastructure-focused investors seeking modest capital appreciation with defensive yield. Value-oriented allocators comfortable with flat revenue growth in exchange for stable mainframe annuity. NOT suitable for growth investors expecting accelerating top-line expansion.

entryExit.text: Average into a $216–$224 zone (20–25% below 52w high). HOLD in $224–$248 range. REDUCE above $248. Key trigger: watsonx AI services revenue run-rate breaks $5bn annually. | BUY: Average into a $216–$224 zone (20–25% below 52w high). | REDUCE above $248.

sources.marketData: Live quote data sourced via DYOR HQ data pipeline (Google Sheets + Yahoo Finance). Fields include price, market capitalisation, 52-week range, P/E, EPS, and volume.
sources.companyFilings: Public company filings, regulatory announcements, investor presentations, and RNS where referenced in the analysis.
